title: Executive Order 11527—Amending Selective Service Regulations

flags.justification: The revised standard for identifying essential activities now requires 'advice of the National Security Council' (Sec. 1) — an added check relative to a standard resting on the Director of Selective Service alone.
The new three-part 'necessary employment' test (Sec. 3) provides specific, checkable criteria in place of a more open prior standard.
Multiple related amendments (occupational, agricultural, and paternity deferment; physical exam rights) all coherently amend one regulatory system.
The new three-part 'necessary employment' test reduces rather than increases vagueness relative to the prior standard, and grandfather clauses are precisely specified.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Standing-procedural-framework category strongly applies: the preamble cites twelve prior amending orders, directly evidencing an extensive pattern of periodic revision.

The newly-created right to request a preinduction physical exam (item 9) may be unilaterally suspended by the Director of Selective Service for particular states or boards based on his own determination that requests would 'adversely affect' processing — an undefined administrative-convenience standard for withdrawing a just-granted procedural right.
The specific criteria and grandfather clauses throughout provide reasonably well-structured, checkable standards.

This implements an already-decided legislative and policy direction (narrowing deferment categories under the Military Selective Service Act) via detailed regulation, rather than asserting a novel executive authority.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
This narrows several existing exemption categories (paternity, agricultural, occupational deferments) rather than broadening any — the opposite of an undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/20 = 5.0%. Part of the broader 1969-1971 Selective Service reform era narrowing deferment categories ahead of the draft lottery system.
